annotations.note: Reading a tab's privileged url (even the add-on's OWN moz-extension:// url), title, or favIconUrl - or filtering tabs.query by them - needs "tabs", a host permission matching the tab's url, or "activeTab"; so "tabs"/"activeTab" is justified whenever the code filters tabs.query by url or title.

Since Thunderbird 154 the add-on's OWN moz-extension:// pages are exempt: reading or filtering by their url/title/favIconUrl needs no permission, so "tabs" justified ONLY by such an own-page read is unused.
